name: GNU Make
description: Classic build automation tool — define targets with dependencies in a Makefile for reproducible project workflows and task orchestration
when_to_use: Standard project automation, defining build/test/deploy targets, when a Makefile already exists in the project

install.command: Pre-installed on most Unix systems || brew install make || sudo apt install make

name: just
description: Modern command runner — like make but focused purely on running project commands without build system complexity, using a simple justfile syntax
when_to_use: Defining project commands (build, test, lint, deploy), replacing Makefiles when you only need task running without dependency graphs

install.command: brew install just || cargo install just

name: Task (go-task)
description: Task runner and build tool written in Go — YAML-based Taskfiles with dependency management, file-based caching, and cross-platform support
when_to_use: Project automation with YAML config, when you want a modern Make alternative with built-in file change detection and task dependencies

name: mise
description: Polyglot runtime and tool version manager — manages Node, Python, Rust, Go, and 200+ tools per-project with .mise.toml, plus a task runner
when_to_use: Managing tool versions across projects (replaces nvm, pyenv, rbenv), ensuring consistent dev environments, running project tasks

install.command: brew install mise || curl https://mise.run | sh

name: direnv
description: Automatic environment variable manager — loads and unloads environment variables based on the current directory using .envrc files
when_to_use: Per-project environment configuration, auto-loading secrets and paths when entering a directory, managing dev environment variables

install.command: brew install direnv || sudo apt install direnv
